user_query:
Please read input/context.yaml and produce a complete change management package tailored to our initiative. Deliverables:

1) Write the full plan to output/change_plan.md. Requirements:
- Start with a one-paragraph Executive Summary.
- Include a 10-section plan with the exact section titles:
  1. Change Overview
  2. Stakeholder Analysis
  3. Communication Plan
  4. Training & Enablement
  5. Resistance Mitigation
  6. Rollout Strategy
  7. Success Metrics & Tracking
  8. Risk Register
  9. Timeline & Milestones
  10. Budget Estimate
- In Stakeholder Analysis, include an ADKAR table (columns: Stakeholder Group, Current State, Impact Level, Likely Resistance, ADKAR Gap) that maps the groups in the YAML.
- In Communication Plan, specify pre-launch, launch, and post-launch communications with channel, audience, owner, and timing.
- In Training & Enablement, include three tiers (Awareness, Competency, Expertise) and assessments, with an AI literacy component suitable for a regulated healthcare setting.
- In Resistance Mitigation, include a table of common resistance patterns and an escalation framework.
- In Rollout Strategy, include a phased approach with the headings “Phase 0 — Pilot”, “Phase 1 — Early Adopters”, “Phase 2 — Majority”, and “Phase 3 — Laggards”, plus a Go/No-Go Checklist with checkboxes. Include compliance gates suitable for HIPAA.
- In Success Metrics & Tracking, define leading and lagging indicators with targets aligned to the YAML, and a tracking cadence.
- In Risk Register, include a table with columns: Risk, Probability, Impact, Mitigation, Owner, Trigger.
- In Timeline & Milestones, build a week-by-week schedule spanning from the YAML start_date through the go_live_date, with owners and dependencies, and clearly mark Go-Live.
- In Budget Estimate, include a table with categories: Training development, Productivity dip, Support staffing, Tools/licenses, Communication, Contingency (15%), with low/high estimates and notes consistent with the YAML constraints.
- Include a Quick-reference checklist (one-page summary of key actions and dates) and an Appendix with an ADKAR assessment template.
- Ensure the plan references our organization by name and the sponsor role, and reflects healthcare compliance (HIPAA) and lessons learned from our previous attempt as described in the YAML.

2) Write a communication calendar to output/communication_calendar.csv with the exact header: week,date,audience,channel,owner,message_title. Include at least 12 rows spanning pre-launch, launch, and post-launch, with entries for clinicians, clinic managers, IT, and compliance. Include a mix of channels (e.g., email, all-hands/town hall, Slack) and owners.

3) Write output/metrics.json capturing the metrics defined in the plan with this structure:
{
  "leading_indicators": [{"name": "...", "target": "..."}, ...],
  "lagging_indicators": [{"name": "...", "target": "..."}, ...],
  "tracking_cadence": {"daily": ["..."], "weekly": ["..."], "monthly": ["..."]}
}

Make the plan concrete and tailored: align the stakeholder mapping, communication, training, risks, timeline, and budget to the specifics in input/context.yaml. Use the provided start_date and go_live_date to anchor all dates and week numbers. All outputs must be written under output/.
